$comment: RELAY v2 FROZEN FRAME CONTRACT — shared golden vectors for the streaming relay.
Both the TS codec test (worker/test/relay-stream.test.ts) and the Go codec test
(agent/relay_vectors_test.go) load THIS SAME file and assert each frame round-trips
to/from the byte-for-byte JSON in `wire`. If you change the wire shape, change it
HERE and both sides re-pass — that is the point of a single fixture.
Wire rules (must hold on both sides):
- One WS message = one frame = one JSON object, UTF-8 text.
- Every frame has { id:string, type:string }. `id` correlates req<->response.
- chunk.data is base64 (RFC 4648 standard alphabet, padded) of a body byte slice.
- head.headers is an ORDERED list of [lowercased-name, value] pairs and MAY contain
duplicate names (e.g. two Set-Cookie). It EXCLUDES hop-by-hop headers:
- STRICT per-id ordering agent->DO: head, then zero+ chunk, then end.
`wire` is the canonical JSON object each side serializes/parses. Field ORDER in
`wire` is NOT significant (JSON object); assert by decoding to a struct/object and
comparing fields, and (for chunks) by decoding base64 and comparing bytes — do not
string-compare the serialized JSON. `bytes_utf8` on chunks is the decoded payload
for convenience; it is NOT part of the wire frame.

req.note: The relayed request. body is the buffered request body as a UTF-8 string. headers is a name->value map (request side stays a map; the DO builds it from Object.fromEntries(req.headers), the agent reads it as map[string]string).

head.note: Sent the INSTANT upstream status+headers are known, BEFORE any body. headers is an ORDERED [name,value] list, lowercased, hop-by-hop excluded, and PRESERVES DUPLICATES (two set-cookie below). This is the SSE-unblocking frame.

chunk_hello.note: First body chunk. data = base64(std,padded) of the 5 bytes "hello".

chunk_world.note: Second body chunk. data = base64(std,padded) of the 6 bytes " world". Concatenating the decoded bytes of chunk_hello + chunk_world yields exactly "hello world".

end.note: Upstream body fully read -> close the stream (controller.close()).

err.note: Error BEFORE head (dial fail -> 502, SSRF reject -> 403). The DO turns this into new Response(message, { status }). Never sent after a head.

reset.note: Abort an in-flight stream. message is optional. DO->agent on idle-timeout-after-head; agent->DO (or DO errors the readable) on its side. message MUST be omitted from the wire when empty (omitempty), so a reset with no message serializes to exactly {id,type}.

reset_no_message.note: A reset with no message — proves `message` is omitted (not "") on the wire.

streamScript.note: A full happy-path response stream for the DO integration test: the ordered sequence of agent->DO frames for one id. Feeding these into the DO's webSocketMessage must produce a Response with status 200, the head headers (dupes preserved, hop-by-hop absent), and a body whose bytes equal "hello world".
